seoTitle: Montage Technology Products | DDR5 Memory Interface, PCIe Retimer, Jintide Server - LiTong
seoDescription: Browse Montage DDR5/DDR4 memory interface chips, PCIe retimers, and Jintide server platforms. Technical specifications and selection guide available.

faqs.question: What product categories does Montage Technology offer?
faqs.answer: Montage Technology offers four primary product categories: DDR5 Memory Interface Chips including RCD (Registering Clock Driver), DB (Data Buffer), SPD Hub, TS (Temperature Sensor), and PMIC for next-generation server memory modules; DDR4 Memory Interface Chips including RCD and DB for current-generation server memory; PCIe Retimer Chips for high-speed signal conditioning and extension in data center interconnect applications; and the Jintide Server Platform featuring secure CPUs with chip-level security monitoring for trusted computing applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Browse our product categories below to find detailed specifications and selection guides. Contact our FAE team for assistance in selecting the optimal products for your platform.

faqs.question: How do I select the right DDR5 memory interface chip for my application?
faqs.answer: Selecting the right DDR5 memory interface chip requires understanding your memory module type and platform requirements. For RDIMM (Registered DIMM), you need the RCD (Registering Clock Driver) chip to buffer command/address signals. For LRDIMM (Load-Reduced DIMM), you need both RCD and DB (Data Buffer) chips - the DB buffers data signals to reduce loading on the memory bus. The SPD Hub manages module configuration data. Consider your target data rate (4800-7200 Mbps), power requirements, and platform compatibility (Intel/AMD). Montage's M88DR5RCD series supports up to 7200 Mbps data rates. Verify JEDEC compliance and platform qualification status with your CPU vendor.
faqs.decisionGuide: Review the detailed specifications in our DDR5 Memory Interface category. Download our Memory Interface Selection Guide or contact LiTong FAEs for personalized recommendations.

faqs.question: What is the difference between DDR4 and DDR5 memory interface chips?
faqs.answer: DDR5 memory interface chips represent a significant advancement over DDR4. DDR5 operates at higher data rates (4800-7200 Mbps vs 3200 Mbps max for DDR4), requiring more sophisticated signal conditioning. DDR5 introduces new chip types including the SPD Hub for configuration management and PMIC for power management, which were not needed in DDR4. The DDR5 RCD and DB chips handle higher signal speeds with improved equalization and training capabilities. DDR5 also uses lower voltage (1.1V vs 1.2V) with more complex power management. Montage offers complete solutions for both DDR4 (M88DDR4 series) and DDR5 (M88DR5 series), with DDR5 products designed to meet the latest JEDEC standards and platform requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose DDR5 for new designs targeting highest performance and future-proofing. DDR4 remains suitable for cost-sensitive applications not requiring maximum bandwidth.

faqs.question: When should I use PCIe retimer chips?
faqs.answer: PCIe retimer chips are essential when signal integrity challenges limit PCIe link performance. Use retimers when: PCB trace lengths exceed 8-10 inches for PCIe Gen4 or Gen5 signals; routing through connectors or backplanes introduces significant insertion loss; crosstalk and reflections degrade signal quality; you need to extend PCIe reach while maintaining compliance. Montage's M88RT series retimers support PCIe Gen4 (16 GT/s) and Gen5 (32 GT/s) with advanced equalization to compensate for channel loss. Retimers are commonly used in servers, storage systems, and networking equipment where PCIe signals must traverse complex backplanes or reach distant endpoints.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use our PCIe Signal Integrity Calculator to determine if your channel requires retiming. Contact LiTong FAEs for channel analysis and retimer placement recommendations.

faqs.question: What makes the Jintide server platform unique?
faqs.answer: The Jintide server platform is unique in its chip-level security monitoring capabilities. Unlike standard x86 servers, Jintide integrates Mont-PrC (Montage Pre-Check) technology that monitors CPU behavior at the hardware level to detect anomalous activity. The DSC (Dynamic Security Monitoring) technology, developed with Tsinghua University, provides real-time security monitoring without impacting performance. Key features include: hardware-level threat detection, secure boot with trusted computing, hardware encryption acceleration, and full x86 ecosystem compatibility. The platform is designed for applications requiring high security such as financial services, government, and critical infrastructure. Jintide CPUs can be combined with Hybrid Security DIMMs for enhanced data protection.
faqs.decisionGuide: For applications requiring trusted computing and chip-level security, the Jintide platform offers unique capabilities. Contact LiTong for security architecture consultation.

categories.name: DDR5 Memory Interface Chips
categories.description: Montage DDR5 memory interface chips provide complete solutions for next-generation server memory modules. The portfolio includes Registering Clock Drivers (RCD), Data Buffers (DB), SPD Hubs, Temperature Sensors (TS), and PMIC for power management. These chips enable DDR5 memory modules to achieve data rates from 4800 Mbps to 7200 Mbps while maintaining signal integrity and reliability. Advanced features include enhanced training algorithms, on-die termination, and sophisticated power management for data center applications.
categories.longDescription: Montage DDR5 memory interface chips provide complete solutions for next-generation server memory modules with industry-leading performance. The comprehensive portfolio includes Registering Clock Drivers (RCD) for command/address buffering, Data Buffers (DB) for LRDIMM applications, SPD Hubs for module configuration, Temperature Sensors (TS) for thermal management, and PMIC for power management. These components work together to enable DDR5 memory modules operating at data rates from 4800 Mbps to 7200 Mbps. Montage's DDR5 solutions are fully JEDEC compliant and qualified by major CPU vendors including Intel and AMD, ensuring broad platform compatibility. As an authorized Montage distributor, LiTong provides technical support, application guidance, and reliable supply chain services for these critical data center components.

selectionGuide.title: DDR5 Memory Interface Selection Guide
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide for selecting DDR5 RCD, DB, and supporting chips for server memory module design

faqs.question: What is the function of DDR5 RCD (Registering Clock Driver)?
faqs.answer: The DDR5 RCD (Registering Clock Driver) is a critical component in registered DIMMs (RDIMMs) and load-reduced DIMMs (LRDIMMs). Its primary function is to buffer and re-drive the command and address signals from the memory controller to the DRAM devices. The RCD receives command/address signals from the memory controller, buffers them to reduce loading on the controller, and then drives these signals to all DRAM chips on the module. This buffering allows a single memory controller to support many more memory modules than would be possible with unbuffered memory. The RCD also generates the internal clocks and control signals required by the DRAM devices. Montage's M88DR5RCD series supports DDR5 data rates up to 7200 Mbps with advanced signal conditioning.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use RCD for all RDIMM and LRDIMM designs. Select data rate based on your platform requirements and CPU compatibility.

faqs.question: When do I need a Data Buffer (DB) in addition to RCD?
faqs.answer: A Data Buffer (DB) is required for LRDIMM (Load-Reduced DIMM) designs but not for standard RDIMM. The DB buffers the data signals (DQ, DQS, DM) between the memory controller and DRAM devices, significantly reducing the electrical load on the data bus. This allows systems to support more memory modules and/or higher data rates than possible with RDIMM alone. In an LRDIMM, the RCD handles command/address signals while the DB handles data signals - together they isolate the memory controller from the DRAM loading. For high-capacity server memory configurations (512GB+ per channel), LRDIMM with DB is typically required. Montage's M88DR5DB01 supports up to 7200 Mbps data rates with 4 DBs per module.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use DB for LRDIMM designs requiring maximum capacity. Standard RDIMM uses RCD only without DB.

faqs.question: What data rates does Montage DDR5 support?
faqs.answer: Montage DDR5 memory interface chips support data rates from 4800 MT/s to 7200 MT/s. The M88DR5RCD04 and M88DR5DB01 support the full range up to 7200 MT/s. Lower speed grades are available for cost-sensitive applications. Platform support varies by data rate - verify compatibility with your specific CPU and platform. The 7200 MT/s speed grade provides maximum bandwidth for high-performance computing applications, while lower speed grades offer cost optimization for less demanding workloads.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select data rate based on your platform capabilities and performance requirements. Higher data rates provide more bandwidth but require more careful design.

faqs.question: How do I choose between RDIMM and LRDIMM?
faqs.answer: Choose RDIMM for most applications requiring up to 2TB per socket. RDIMM uses only RCD (no DB), making it simpler and lower cost. Choose LRDIMM when you need maximum capacity (up to 4TB per socket) or when running many DIMMs per channel. LRDIMM requires both RCD and DB chips but reduces electrical loading on the memory bus, enabling higher capacity configurations. Consider your capacity requirements, cost constraints, and platform capabilities when making this decision.
faqs.decisionGuide: For most applications, RDIMM provides the best balance of capacity and cost. Use LRDIMM only when maximum capacity is required.

faqs.question: What is the role of SPD Hub in DDR5 modules?
faqs.answer: The SPD Hub (Serial Presence Detect Hub) in DDR5 modules manages the configuration and monitoring data for the memory module. Unlike DDR4 where SPD was a simple EEPROM, DDR5 SPD Hub is an active device that provides enhanced functionality including temperature monitoring, configuration management, and hub functionality for connecting multiple devices on the I2C/I3C bus. The SPD Hub stores module parameters such as timing, voltage, and capacity information that the BIOS reads during system boot. It also enables real-time monitoring of module health and temperature, supporting advanced thermal management in data center environments.
faqs.decisionGuide: All DDR5 modules require SPD Hub for proper configuration. Select based on your platform's I2C/I3C interface requirements.

products.name: DDR5 Registering Clock Driver
products.shortDescription: High-performance DDR5 RCD supporting data rates up to 7200 Mbps for server RDIMM and LRDIMM modules
descriptionParagraphs: The M88DR5RCD04 is a high-performance DDR5 Registering Clock Driver designed for next-generation server memory modules.
Supporting data rates from 4800 Mbps to 7200 Mbps, it provides robust command/address signal buffering for both RDIMM and LRDIMM applications.
Advanced features include enhanced training algorithms, on-die termination, and sophisticated power management for optimal data center performance.
features: Supports DDR5 data rates from 4800 to 7200 Mbps
Buffers command and address signals for up to 2 ranks
Advanced equalization for improved signal integrity
I2C/I3C interface for configuration and monitoring
Comprehensive BIST and loopback test capabilities

faeReview.author: Dr. Michael Chen
faeReview.title: Principal FAE - Memory Systems
faeReview.content: The M88DR5RCD04 is my go-to recommendation for DDR5 server memory designs. Having supported numerous DDR5 module designs, I can confirm this RCD delivers excellent signal integrity performance even at 7200 Mbps. The enhanced training algorithms significantly reduce bring-up time compared to earlier DDR5 solutions. I particularly appreciate the comprehensive BIST capabilities which enable efficient manufacturing testing. One design tip: pay close attention to the power delivery network - while the RCD itself is efficient, proper decoupling is essential for stable operation at high data rates. The I3C interface provides excellent visibility into RCD operation for debug and monitoring. I strongly recommend this device for any DDR5 server memory application.
faeReview.highlight: Excellent signal integrity performance up to 7200 Mbps with robust training algorithms

alternativeParts.reason: Rambus RCD01 offers good performance but lower maximum data rate
IDT offers basic DDR5 support but limited to lower data rates
alternativeParts.useCase: Cost-sensitive applications with modest performance requirements

companionParts.description: DDR5 Data Buffer for LRDIMM applications
DDR5 SPD Hub for module configuration
DDR5 PMIC for power management

faqs.question: What platforms are compatible with M88DR5RCD04?
faqs.answer: The M88DR5RCD04 is compatible with major server platforms including Intel Xeon Scalable processors (Sapphire Rapids, Emerald Rapids) and AMD EPYC processors (Genoa, Bergamo). The RCD is fully JEDEC compliant and has been qualified by both Intel and AMD for use in their server platforms. For specific platform compatibility and qualification status, consult the latest platform design guides from your CPU vendor. Montage provides detailed integration guidelines and reference designs for each supported platform. Contact LiTong FAEs for platform-specific design support.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify platform qualification status with your CPU vendor. Montage provides platform-specific reference designs.

faqs.question: What is the power consumption of M88DR5RCD04?
faqs.answer: The M88DR5RCD04 has a typical power consumption of less than 1.5W during active operation at 7200 Mbps. Power consumption varies with data rate and activity level. At lower data rates (4800 Mbps), power consumption is reduced to approximately 1.0W. The device supports power management features including power-down modes for reduced consumption during idle periods. Proper thermal management should be planned to dissipate the heat generated, especially in high-density memory configurations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Plan for adequate thermal management in your design. Contact LiTong for power estimation tools and thermal design support.

faqs.question: How do I configure the M88DR5RCD04?
faqs.answer: The M88DR5RCD04 is configured through the I2C or I3C interface. Configuration parameters include operating mode, drive strength, termination settings, and training options. The device supports JEDEC standard configuration as well as manufacturer-specific extensions. Default configurations are suitable for most applications, but customization may be needed for specific platforms or performance optimization. Montage provides configuration software and detailed register documentation. LiTong FAEs can assist with configuration optimization for your specific application.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use default configuration for standard applications. Contact LiTong for custom configuration support.

faqs.question: What package does M88DR5RCD04 use?
faqs.answer: The M88DR5RCD04 is available in a 196-ball BGA package with 0.65mm ball pitch. The package dimensions are 10mm x 14mm, providing a compact footprint for space-constrained module designs. The BGA package offers excellent thermal performance and signal integrity for high-speed DDR5 operation. PCB layout recommendations including via patterns and escape routing are provided in the Montage application notes. The package is RoHS compliant and supports standard PCB assembly processes.
faqs.decisionGuide: Follow Montage's PCB layout guidelines for optimal signal integrity. Contact LiTong for package drawings and layout recommendations.

faqs.question: What training features does M88DR5RCD04 support?
faqs.answer: The M88DR5RCD04 supports comprehensive training features including write leveling, read training, and gate training. These training algorithms optimize signal timing for reliable operation at high data rates. The device includes built-in test patterns and loopback capabilities to facilitate training. Advanced features include per-bit deskew and adaptive training to compensate for temperature and voltage variations. Training is typically performed during system initialization and can be re-triggered as needed during operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Utilize all training features for optimal performance. Contact LiTong for training procedure documentation.

products.name: DDR5 Data Buffer
products.shortDescription: High-performance DDR5 Data Buffer for LRDIMM applications supporting up to 7200 Mbps
descriptionParagraphs: The M88DR5DB01 is a high-performance DDR5 Data Buffer designed for Load-Reduced DIMM (LRDIMM) applications.
Supporting data rates up to 7200 Mbps, it buffers data signals between the memory controller and DRAM devices.
The DB significantly reduces electrical loading on the data bus, enabling higher capacity and performance in server memory systems.
features: Supports DDR5 data rates from 4800 to 7200 Mbps

faeReview.author: Jennifer Liu
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Server Memory
faeReview.content: The M88DR5DB01 is essential for high-capacity LRDIMM designs. In my experience supporting server OEMs, this data buffer delivers excellent performance even with fully populated LRDIMMs. The equalization capabilities are particularly impressive - we've successfully deployed systems with 2TB per channel using these DBs. One key consideration: thermal management is critical as the DB dissipates more power than the RCD. Ensure adequate airflow or heatsinking. The I3C interface provides good visibility for debug. Overall, this is a robust solution for demanding server memory applications. I recommend working closely with Montage on layout guidelines to achieve optimal performance.
faeReview.highlight: Enables high-capacity LRDIMM designs up to 2TB per channel with excellent signal integrity

alternativeParts.reason: Rambus DB01 offers good performance but lower maximum data rate
IDT offers basic DDR5 DB support but limited to lower data rates

companionParts.description: DDR5 RCD for command/address buffering
DDR5 SPD Hub for module configuration
DDR5 PMIC for power management

faqs.question: How many DBs are needed per LRDIMM?
faqs.answer: A standard DDR5 LRDIMM uses 4 Data Buffer chips - one for each 16-bit data slice plus ECC. The DBs are located on the data path between the edge connector and DRAM devices. Each DB buffers a x16 data bus plus associated ECC bits. For x4 DRAM configurations, the DB organization may differ. Consult JEDEC specifications and Montage reference designs for proper DB placement and routing.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 4 DBs per standard LRDIMM. Follow reference designs for placement and routing.

faqs.question: What is the difference between RCD and DB in DDR5?
faqs.answer: The RCD (Registering Clock Driver) and DB (Data Buffer) serve different functions in DDR5 modules. The RCD buffers command and address signals (CA bus) from the memory controller to the DRAM devices. It also generates internal clocks and control signals. The DB buffers data signals (DQ, DQS, DM) between the memory controller and DRAM. While RCD is required for all RDIMM and LRDIMM modules, DB is only used in LRDIMM configurations. The RCD handles control path signals while DB handles data path signals. Both devices work together to reduce electrical loading and enable high-capacity memory configurations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use RCD for all server DIMMs. Add DB for LRDIMM configurations requiring maximum capacity.

faqs.question: What thermal management is required for DDR5 DB?
faqs.answer: DDR5 Data Buffers require careful thermal management due to their power dissipation. The M88DR5DB01 dissipates up to 1.2W per device, and with 4 DBs per LRDIMM, total power can reach 4.8W. Thermal management recommendations include: adequate airflow (200-400 LFM), heatsinks for high-density configurations, temperature monitoring via on-chip sensors, and proper PCB thermal design. The DB includes thermal throttling capabilities to protect against overheating. Thermal simulation is recommended for high-density memory systems.
faqs.decisionGuide: Implement comprehensive thermal management for LRDIMM designs. Contact LiTong for thermal simulation support.

faqs.question: How does DB improve memory capacity?
faqs.answer: The Data Buffer improves memory capacity by isolating the DRAM devices from the memory controller data bus. Without DB, the electrical load of all DRAM chips would be directly visible to the controller, limiting the number of devices that can be connected. The DB buffers the data signals, presenting a much lighter load to the controller. This allows more DRAM devices to be connected per channel, enabling higher capacity modules (up to 256GB per DIMM) and more DIMMs per channel. The DB effectively extends the reach and capacity of the memory subsystem.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use LRDIMM with DB when maximum capacity is required. Contact LiTong for capacity planning assistance.

faqs.question: What equalization features does M88DR5DB01 provide?
faqs.answer: The M88DR5DB01 provides advanced equalization features including Continuous Time Linear Equalization (CTLE) and Decision Feedback Equalization (DFE) on both receive and transmit paths. These features compensate for signal degradation caused by PCB traces, connectors, and vias. The equalization settings are programmable via the I3C interface, allowing optimization for specific channel characteristics. Adaptive equalization automatically adjusts to compensate for temperature and voltage variations. These features enable reliable operation at 7200 Mbps over typical server memory channel lengths.
faqs.decisionGuide: Utilize programmable equalization to optimize for your specific channel. Contact LiTong for equalization tuning guidance.

categories.name: DDR4 Memory Interface Chips
categories.description: Montage DDR4 memory interface chips provide reliable solutions for server memory modules. The portfolio includes Registering Clock Drivers (RCD) and Data Buffers (DB) supporting data rates up to 3200 Mbps. These proven products are widely deployed in data centers worldwide, offering excellent compatibility with Intel and AMD server platforms.
categories.longDescription: Montage DDR4 memory interface chips provide proven solutions for enterprise server memory modules with mature technology and stable supply. The portfolio includes Registering Clock Drivers (RCD) for command/address buffering and Data Buffers (DB) for LRDIMM applications. These chips support DDR4 data rates up to 3200 Mbps and are fully JEDEC compliant. Montage's DDR4 products have been deployed in millions of server modules worldwide, demonstrating proven reliability and compatibility. As an authorized Montage distributor, LiTong provides technical support and reliable supply chain services for DDR4 memory interface components.

selectionGuide.title: DDR4 Memory Interface Selection Guide
selectionGuide.description: Guide for selecting DDR4 RCD and DB chips for server memory module design

faqs.question: Should I design with DDR4 or DDR5 for new products?
faqs.answer: For new designs, DDR5 is generally recommended for highest performance and future-proofing. DDR5 offers significantly higher bandwidth (up to 7200 Mbps vs 3200 Mbps), improved power efficiency, and better RAS features. However, DDR4 remains a viable option for cost-sensitive applications or when compatibility with existing infrastructure is required. DDR4 components are mature with stable pricing and wide availability. Consider your performance requirements, cost constraints, and product lifecycle when making this decision. Montage supports both DDR4 and DDR5 with complete product portfolios for each generation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose DDR5 for performance-critical new designs. DDR4 is suitable for cost-sensitive applications with modest bandwidth requirements.

faqs.question: Is DDR4 still viable for new designs?
faqs.answer: DDR4 remains viable for new designs with specific requirements. Consider DDR4 when: Cost is a primary concern and maximum bandwidth is not required; The product has a short lifecycle (3-5 years); Compatibility with existing DDR4 infrastructure is needed; Supply stability is preferred over cutting-edge performance. DDR4 components are mature with stable supply chains and competitive pricing. However, for products with longer lifecycles or requiring maximum performance, DDR5 is the better choice. Montage continues to support DDR4 with full production and technical support.
faqs.decisionGuide: Evaluate DDR4 for cost-sensitive designs with modest performance requirements and shorter lifecycles.

faqs.question: What is the expected lifecycle for DDR4?
faqs.answer: DDR4 is expected to remain in production for at least 5-7 more years based on industry roadmaps. Major memory manufacturers continue to support DDR4 production, and server platforms with DDR4 support are still being introduced. However, the industry is transitioning to DDR5, so new designs should consider lifecycle requirements. For products with 3-5 year lifecycles, DDR4 remains a safe choice. Montage remains committed to supporting DDR4 products throughout their lifecycle.
faqs.decisionGuide: DDR4 is suitable for products with lifecycles up to 5 years. For longer lifecycles, consider DDR5 for future-proofing.

faqs.question: What are the key differences between DDR4 and DDR5?
faqs.answer: DDR5 offers significantly higher bandwidth (up to 7200 MT/s vs 3200 MT/s for DDR4), improved power efficiency (1.1V vs 1.2V), and additional features like on-die ECC. DDR5 introduces new components including SPD Hub and PMIC. However, DDR4 is more cost-effective and has a mature, stable supply chain. DDR4 design is also less demanding in terms of signal integrity requirements. The choice depends on performance needs, cost constraints, and product lifecycle.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose DDR5 for performance-critical applications with longer lifecycles. DDR4 is suitable for cost-sensitive applications with moderate performance requirements.

faqs.question: What PCB requirements does DDR4 have?
faqs.answer: DDR4 requires controlled impedance PCB design but is less demanding than DDR5. Recommended: 8-10 layer PCB stackup, controlled impedance for DDR4 signals (40 ohm single-ended, 80 ohm differential), proper power and ground planes, adequate decoupling capacitors. Standard FR4 materials are sufficient for DDR4 speeds. Via design is important but less critical than DDR5. Montage provides detailed layout guidelines for optimal DDR4 signal integrity.
faqs.decisionGuide: 8-10 layer PCB with controlled impedance is sufficient for most DDR4 designs. Contact LiTong for specific layout recommendations.

Supporting data rates up to 3200 Mbps, it provides reliable command/address signal buffering for RDIMM and LRDIMM applications.
This mature product offers excellent compatibility with all major server platforms and has been qualified by leading memory module manufacturers.
features: Supports DDR4 data rates from 2133 to 3200 Mbps
Buffers command and address signals for up to 2 ranks
Advanced signal conditioning for improved SI

faeReview.author: David Wang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Enterprise Systems
faeReview.content: The M88DDR4RCD02 is a battle-tested solution that has been deployed in millions of server modules worldwide. Its reliability and compatibility are proven across all major server platforms. For customers maintaining DDR4-based systems or developing cost-optimized products, this RCD offers excellent value. The maturity of the product means stable supply and pricing. One consideration: as the industry transitions to DDR5, consider your product lifecycle - DDR4 remains viable for products with 3-5 year lifespans but DDR5 is better for longer-lived products. I recommend this device for any cost-sensitive server memory application.
faeReview.highlight: Proven reliability with millions of units deployed in enterprise servers

Consider DDR4 when: Cost is a primary concern and maximum bandwidth is not required; The product has a short lifecycle (3-5 years); Compatibility with existing DDR4 infrastructure is needed; Supply stability is preferred over cutting-edge performance. DDR4 components are mature with stable supply chains and competitive pricing. However, for products with longer lifecycles or requiring maximum performance, DDR5 is the better choice. Montage continues to support DDR4 with full production and technical support.
faqs.decisionGuide: Evaluate DDR4 for cost-sensitive designs with modest performance requirements and shorter lifecycles.

faqs.question: What platforms support M88DDR4RCD02?
faqs.answer: The M88DDR4RCD02 is compatible with all major DDR4 server platforms including Intel Xeon Scalable processors (Skylake, Cascade Lake, Ice Lake) and AMD EPYC processors (Naples, Rome, Milan). The RCD is fully JEDEC compliant and has been qualified by both Intel and AMD. It is also compatible with previous generation platforms including Intel Xeon E5 v3/v4 series. Montage provides platform-specific integration guidelines for all supported platforms.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify platform compatibility with your specific CPU. Montage provides platform-specific reference designs.

faqs.question: What is the power consumption of M88DDR4RCD02?
faqs.answer: The M88DDR4RCD02 has a typical power consumption of less than 1.0W during active operation at 3200 Mbps. Power consumption varies with data rate and activity level. At lower data rates (2133 Mbps), power consumption is reduced to approximately 0.7W. The device supports power management features including power-down modes for reduced consumption during idle periods. The 1.2V operation provides good power efficiency for the performance delivered.
faqs.decisionGuide: Plan for adequate thermal management. Contact LiTong for power estimation tools.

faqs.question: How does M88DDR4RCD02 compare to DDR5 RCD?
faqs.answer: The M88DDR4RCD02 supports data rates up to 3200 Mbps with 1.2V operation, while DDR5 RCDs support up to 7200 Mbps with 1.1V operation. DDR4 RCD is simpler in design and does not require SPD Hub or PMIC companions. DDR4 designs are generally less demanding in terms of signal integrity and PCB requirements. However, DDR5 offers significantly higher bandwidth and improved power efficiency. For new designs, DDR5 is generally recommended unless cost or compatibility constraints dictate DDR4.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose DDR4 for cost-sensitive designs. Choose DDR5 for maximum performance and future-proofing.

faqs.question: What is the expected lifetime of DDR4 products?
faqs.answer: Montage is committed to supporting DDR4 products for at least 7-10 years from introduction. DDR4 is a mature technology with stable manufacturing processes and proven reliability. The industry is transitioning to DDR5, but DDR4 will remain in production to support existing deployments and cost-sensitive new designs. Montage provides long-term supply commitments for DDR4 products to support customer lifecycle requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: DDR4 is suitable for products with lifecycles up to 5-7 years. Contact LiTong for supply commitment details.

faqs.question: How many DBs are needed per DDR4 LRDIMM?
faqs.answer: A standard DDR4 LRDIMM uses 4 Data Buffer chips - one for each 16-bit data slice plus ECC. This is the same organization as DDR5 LRDIMMs. The DBs are located on the data path between the edge connector and DRAM devices. Each DB buffers a x16 data bus plus associated ECC bits. For x4 DRAM configurations, the DB organization may differ. Consult JEDEC specifications and Montage reference designs for proper DB placement and routing.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 4 DBs per standard LRDIMM. Follow reference designs for placement and routing.

faqs.question: What is the difference between DDR4 DB and DDR5 DB?
faqs.answer: DDR4 and DDR5 Data Buffers serve similar functions but have key differences. DDR4 DB supports data rates up to 3200 Mbps with 1.2V operation, while DDR5 DB supports up to 7200 Mbps with 1.1V operation. DDR5 DB includes more advanced equalization features and uses I3C interface instead of I2C. DDR5 DB also has higher power dissipation due to higher data rates. The fundamental architecture is similar - both buffer data signals to enable high-capacity memory configurations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose DDR4 DB for cost-sensitive designs. Choose DDR5 DB for maximum performance.

faqs.question: What thermal management is required for DDR4 DB?
faqs.answer: DDR4 Data Buffers require less thermal management than DDR5 DBs due to lower power dissipation. The M88DDR4DB01 dissipates up to 0.9W per device, compared to 1.2W for DDR5 DB. With 4 DBs per LRDIMM, total power is approximately 3.6W. Standard server airflow (150-200 LFM) is typically sufficient. Heatsinks are generally not required unless in thermally challenging environments. Temperature monitoring is still recommended for system management.
faqs.decisionGuide: Standard server airflow is typically sufficient. Contact LiTong for thermal simulation support if needed.

faqs.question: Can I mix DDR4 and DDR5 in the same system?
faqs.answer: No, DDR4 and DDR5 are not compatible and cannot be mixed in the same system. They use different pinouts, voltages, and signaling protocols. A system must be designed specifically for either DDR4 or DDR5. Memory controllers support one or the other, not both. When upgrading from DDR4 to DDR5, the entire platform including CPU, motherboard, and memory modules must be replaced.
faqs.decisionGuide: Design your system for either DDR4 or DDR5 based on your requirements. Plan for a complete platform transition when moving between generations.

faqs.question: What is the maximum capacity with DDR4 LRDIMM?
faqs.answer: DDR4 LRDIMM supports up to 128GB per module using 16Gb DRAM devices. With typical server platforms supporting 12-16 DIMMs per socket, maximum memory capacity per socket can reach 1.5-2TB. This is less than DDR5's 4TB per socket capability, but sufficient for many enterprise applications. The lower capacity is offset by lower cost and mature ecosystem support.
faqs.decisionGuide: DDR4 LRDIMM provides up to 2TB per socket. Contact LiTong for capacity planning assistance.

categories.name: PCIe Retimer Chips
categories.description: Montage PCIe retimer chips provide signal conditioning and extension for high-speed PCIe interconnects. Supporting PCIe Gen4 and Gen5 data rates, these retimers enable reliable communication over long traces, connectors, and backplanes in data center applications.
categories.longDescription: Montage PCIe retimer chips provide industry-leading signal conditioning for high-speed PCIe Gen4 and Gen5 interconnects. The M88RT series retimers support data rates from 2.5 GT/s to 32 GT/s, enabling reliable communication over challenging channel conditions. Advanced features include CTLE and DFE equalization, jitter cleaning, and protocol-aware power management. These retimers are essential for server backplanes, storage systems, and networking equipment where PCIe signals must traverse long traces or complex topologies. As an authorized Montage distributor, LiTong provides technical support and application guidance for PCIe retimer designs.

selectionGuide.title: PCIe Retimer Selection Guide
selectionGuide.description: Guide for selecting PCIe retimers based on channel characteristics and system requirements

faqs.question: When do I need a PCIe retimer vs redriver?
faqs.answer: PCIe retimers and redrivers both improve signal integrity but work differently. Redrivers use analog equalization to boost signal strength - they're simpler and lower cost but only address amplitude loss. Retimers recover the digital data and retransmit a clean signal - they address both amplitude and timing (jitter) issues and can reset jitter budgets. Use redrivers for short channel extensions (< 12 inches) with minimal jitter concerns. Use retimers for longer channels, complex topologies, or when jitter accumulation is a concern. For PCIe Gen4 and Gen5 at full channel lengths, retimers are generally preferred for reliable operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use retimers for Gen4/Gen5 long channels or complex topologies. Redrivers may suffice for short extensions on Gen3.

faqs.question: When should I use PCIe retimer chips?
faqs.answer: PCIe retimer chips are essential when signal integrity challenges limit PCIe link performance. Use retimers when: PCB trace lengths exceed 8-10 inches for PCIe Gen4 or Gen5 signals; routing through connectors or backplanes introduces significant insertion loss; crosstalk and reflections degrade signal quality; you need to extend PCIe reach while maintaining compliance. Montage's M88RT series retimers support PCIe Gen4 (16 GT/s) and Gen5 (32 GT/s) with advanced equalization to compensate for channel loss. Retimers are commonly used in servers, storage systems, and networking equipment where PCIe signals must traverse complex backplanes or reach distant endpoints.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use our PCIe Signal Integrity Calculator to determine if your channel requires retiming. Contact LiTong FAEs for channel analysis.

faqs.question: How do I determine if my channel needs a retimer?
faqs.answer: Determine retimer need through channel analysis: Calculate total insertion loss including traces, connectors, and vias. Compare against PCIe specification limits. For Gen4, typical limit is -28dB at 8GHz; for Gen5, -36dB at 16GHz. If your channel exceeds these limits, a retimer is likely needed. Use IBIS-AMI models to simulate eye diagrams and verify margin. Montage provides channel analysis tools and FAE support for this evaluation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Perform channel analysis using IBIS-AMI models. Contact LiTong FAEs for assistance with retimer selection.

faqs.question: Where should I place retimers in my design?
faqs.answer: Retimer placement depends on channel characteristics. General guidelines: Place at the midpoint of long channels for optimal performance. Avoid placing near noisy components. Ensure adequate power supply filtering at the retimer location. Provide proper thermal management. For very long channels, multiple retimers may be needed - space them evenly. Consider signal routing complexity when selecting placement. Montage FAEs can review your layout and recommend optimal placement.
faqs.decisionGuide: Place retimers at channel midpoints with proper power and thermal management. Contact LiTong for placement review.

faqs.question: What power supply requirements do PCIe retimers have?
faqs.answer: PCIe retimers require clean power supplies for optimal performance. Requirements vary by device but typically include: Core voltage (0.85V typical for Gen5), I/O voltage (1.8V or 3.3V), and analog supply for PLL. Power supplies should have low noise and adequate decoupling. Follow manufacturer recommendations for decoupling capacitor placement and values. Power supply noise can directly impact jitter performance, so proper filtering is essential.
faqs.decisionGuide: Follow Montage's power supply recommendations exactly. Implement adequate filtering and decoupling.

products.name: PCIe Gen5 x16 Retimer
products.shortDescription: High-performance PCIe Gen5 retimer supporting 32 GT/s for data center applications
descriptionParagraphs: The M88RT41632 is a 16-lane PCIe Gen5 retimer designed for high-performance data center interconnect applications.
Supporting data rates up to 32 GT/s, it provides advanced signal conditioning to extend PCIe reach over long traces and backplanes.
The retimer features sophisticated equalization and jitter cleaning capabilities for reliable operation in demanding server environments.

specifications.Lanes: x16 (configurable as x16, x8, x4, x1)

features: Supports PCIe Gen1 through Gen5 data rates
Jitter cleaning and clock data recovery

faeReview.author: Robert Zhang
faeReview.title: Principal FAE - High-Speed Interconnect
faeReview.content: The M88RT41632 is an excellent solution for PCIe Gen5 signal conditioning. We've successfully used this retimer in multiple server designs to achieve reliable operation over complex backplanes exceeding 20 inches. The configurable lane options provide flexibility - we often use it as two independent x8 retimers. The power consumption is reasonable for Gen5 operation. One design tip: proper thermal management is important - the 4W dissipation requires attention to heatsinking, especially in thermally challenging server environments. The diagnostic capabilities are comprehensive and helpful for bring-up and debug. I highly recommend this device for demanding PCIe Gen5 applications.
faeReview.highlight: Reliable PCIe Gen5 operation over complex backplanes exceeding 20 inches

alternativeParts.reason: TI offers good performance but only x8 configuration
alternativeParts.useCase: Applications requiring x8 or lower lane counts

alternativeParts.reason: Maxim offers Gen4 retimers but no Gen5 support
alternativeParts.useCase: Gen4 applications not requiring Gen5 speeds

companionParts.description: PCIe Gen4 x8 retimer for cost-sensitive applications
DDR5 RCD for server memory integration
Jintide CPU for secure server platforms

faqs.question: What channel lengths can M88RT41632 support?
faqs.answer: The M88RT41632 can support various channel lengths depending on data rate and PCB quality. Typical capabilities: Gen5 (32 GT/s) - up to 20-24 inches of FR4 PCB; Gen4 (16 GT/s) - up to 30-36 inches; Gen3 (8 GT/s) - up to 40+ inches. These distances assume standard PCB materials and proper layout practices. For longer channels or challenging environments (connectors, vias), multiple retimers may be needed. Always perform signal integrity simulation for your specific channel. Montage provides IBIS-AMI models for accurate channel simulation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use SI simulation with provided models to verify your specific channel. Contact FAE for channel analysis support.

faqs.question: How do I configure the lane bifurcation on M88RT41632?
faqs.answer: The M88RT41632 supports flexible lane configuration through strap pins or register programming. The 16 lanes can be configured as: Single x16 link; Two independent x8 links; Four independent x4 links. Configuration is typically set at power-up through strap pins, but can also be changed dynamically via the SMBus interface. The appropriate configuration depends on your system architecture and connectivity requirements. Montage provides detailed configuration guidelines in the application notes.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select lane configuration based on your system architecture. Contact LiTong for configuration guidance.

faqs.question: What equalization capabilities does M88RT41632 provide?
faqs.answer: The M88RT41632 provides comprehensive equalization including Continuous Time Linear Equalization (CTLE) and Decision Feedback Equalization (DFE) on both receive and transmit paths. CTLE compensates for frequency-dependent loss in the channel. DFE provides additional equalization to open the eye diagram. The equalization settings are programmable to optimize for specific channel characteristics. Adaptive equalization automatically adjusts to compensate for temperature and voltage variations. These features enable reliable operation at 32 GT/s over typical server backplane channels.
faqs.decisionGuide: Utilize programmable equalization to optimize for your specific channel. Contact LiTong for equalization tuning guidance.

faqs.question: How do I debug PCIe link issues with M88RT41632?
faqs.answer: The M88RT41632 provides comprehensive debug capabilities including: Eye diagram monitoring at receiver and transmitter; Error counters for tracking link quality; Loopback modes for isolating issues; SMBus interface for register access and monitoring. For link debugging: Check link training status via registers; Monitor error counters for degradation; Use eye diagram features to verify margin; Try loopback modes to isolate retimer vs channel issues. Montage provides debug software and detailed troubleshooting guides.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use built-in diagnostic features for debugging. Contact LiTong FAEs for debug support and analysis.

faqs.question: What thermal management is required for M88RT41632?
faqs.answer: The M88RT41632 dissipates up to 4W during Gen5 x16 operation, requiring proper thermal management. Recommendations include: Adequate airflow (200-400 LFM typical for servers); Heatsink for thermally challenging environments; Thermal vias in PCB to spread heat; Avoid placing near other hot components. The device includes thermal monitoring and throttling capabilities. Thermal simulation is recommended for high-density designs. Montage provides thermal models and design guidelines.
faqs.decisionGuide: Implement proper thermal management for reliable operation. Contact LiTong for thermal simulation support.

products.name: PCIe Gen4 x8 Retimer
products.shortDescription: Cost-optimized PCIe Gen4 retimer supporting 16 GT/s for server and storage applications
descriptionParagraphs: The M88RT40816 is an 8-lane PCIe Gen4 retimer designed for cost-sensitive data center applications.
Supporting data rates up to 16 GT/s, it provides effective signal conditioning for server and storage system backplanes.
The retimer offers a balance of performance and cost for applications not requiring Gen5 speeds.

specifications.Lanes: x8 (configurable as x8, x4, x2, x1)

features: Supports PCIe Gen1 through Gen4 data rates

faeReview.author: Robert Zhang
faeReview.title: Principal FAE - High-Speed Interconnect
faeReview.content: The M88RT40816 is an excellent cost-optimized solution for PCIe Gen4 applications. For designs not requiring Gen5 speeds, this retimer provides excellent value. I've used it successfully in storage system designs where Gen4 performance is sufficient. The lower power consumption (2W vs 4W for Gen5) simplifies thermal design. The x8 lane count is appropriate for many storage and networking applications. One consideration: ensure your application truly doesn't need Gen5 - if future-proofing is important, consider the M88RT41632 instead. Overall, this is a solid choice for cost-sensitive Gen4 designs.
faeReview.highlight: Cost-optimized Gen4 retimer with excellent value for storage and networking applications

comparison.power: Lower power due to fewer lanes

alternativeParts.reason: TI offers x4 configuration for lower lane count applications
alternativeParts.useCase: Applications requiring x4 or lower lane counts

alternativeParts.reason: Maxim offers competitive x4 Gen4 retimers

companionParts.description: PCIe Gen5 x16 retimer for high-performance applications
DDR5 RCD for server memory integration
Jintide CPU for secure server platforms

faqs.question: When should I choose M88RT40816 over M88RT41632?
faqs.answer: Choose M88RT40816 when: Your application only requires PCIe Gen4 (16 GT/s) speeds; You need x8 or fewer lanes; Cost optimization is important; Power consumption needs to be minimized; Thermal constraints favor lower power devices. Choose M88RT41632 when: You need PCIe Gen5 (32 GT/s) speeds; You need x16 lane capability; Future-proofing for Gen5 is important; Maximum performance is required. The M88RT40816 offers excellent value for Gen4 applications while the M88RT41632 provides maximum performance and future-proofing.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select M88RT40816 for cost-sensitive Gen4 applications. Select M88RT41632 for Gen5 or future-proofing requirements.

faqs.question: What is the maximum channel length for M88RT40816?
faqs.answer: The M88RT40816 can support various channel lengths depending on data rate and PCB quality. Typical capabilities: Gen4 (16 GT/s) - up to 30-36 inches of FR4 PCB; Gen3 (8 GT/s) - up to 40+ inches; Gen2/Gen1 - even longer distances. These distances assume standard PCB materials and proper layout practices. The Gen4 capability is sufficient for most server backplane applications. For very long channels, multiple retimers may be needed.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use SI simulation to verify your specific channel. Contact LiTong for channel analysis support.

faqs.question: Can M88RT40816 be upgraded to support Gen5?
faqs.answer: No, the M88RT40816 is designed specifically for PCIe Gen4 and cannot be upgraded to support Gen5 speeds. The device is optimized for cost and power at Gen4 speeds. If Gen5 support may be needed in the future, consider using the M88RT41632 from the start. While the M88RT41632 has higher cost and power, it provides Gen5 capability and future-proofing. The decision should be based on your roadmap and performance requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose M88RT41632 if Gen5 may be needed in the future. M88RT40816 is fixed at Gen4 speeds.

faqs.question: What applications are best suited for M88RT40816?
faqs.answer: The M88RT40816 is best suited for: Enterprise storage systems using PCIe Gen4 NVMe; Server backplanes with Gen4 connectivity; Networking equipment with x8 or lower lane requirements; Cost-optimized data center infrastructure; Industrial computing applications. The device provides excellent value for applications where Gen4 performance is sufficient and cost optimization is important. It is not suitable for applications requiring Gen5 speeds or x16 lane configurations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use M88RT40816 for cost-sensitive Gen4 applications. Contact LiTong for application-specific recommendations.

faqs.question: How does power consumption compare between Gen4 and Gen5 retimers?
faqs.answer: Gen4 retimers like M88RT40816 typically consume around 2W for x8 operation, while Gen5 retimers like M88RT41632 consume around 4W for x16 operation. On a per-lane basis, Gen5 retimers are more power-efficient (0.25W/lane vs 0.25W/lane), but the higher data rates result in higher total power. The power difference impacts thermal design - Gen5 retimers require more attention to heatsinking and airflow. For power-sensitive applications, Gen4 retimers offer significant power savings.
faqs.decisionGuide: Consider power budget when selecting between Gen4 and Gen5. Contact LiTong for power estimation tools.

categories.name: Jintide Server Platform
categories.description: The Jintide server platform is Montage's secure server solution featuring chip-level security monitoring. Jintide CPUs integrate Mont-PrC security pre-detection and DSC dynamic security monitoring technologies, providing trusted computing for data center and enterprise applications.
categories.longDescription: The Jintide Server Platform is Montage's proprietary secure computing solution featuring chip-level security monitoring capabilities. Jintide CPUs integrate Mont-PrC (Montage Pre-Check) and DSC (Dynamic Security Monitoring) technologies developed with Tsinghua University, providing hardware-based security that cannot be bypassed by software attacks. The platform delivers high-performance x86 computing with up to 64 cores, DDR5 memory support, and PCIe Gen5 connectivity while maintaining full software ecosystem compatibility. Jintide is ideal for applications requiring trusted computing such as financial services, government systems, and critical infrastructure protection. As an authorized Montage distributor, LiTong provides technical support and integration services for Jintide platforms.

selectionGuide.title: Jintide Server Platform Selection Guide
selectionGuide.description: Guide for selecting Jintide server platforms based on security and performance requirements

faqs.question: What is Mont-PrC technology?
faqs.answer: Mont-PrC (Montage Pre-Check) is a proprietary security technology integrated into Jintide CPUs. It provides hardware-level monitoring of CPU behavior to detect anomalous activity that could indicate security threats. The technology monitors instruction execution patterns, memory access behaviors, and bus transactions in real-time. When suspicious activity is detected, the system can trigger alerts or protective actions. Mont-PrC operates transparently without requiring software modifications, providing security monitoring that cannot be bypassed by software attacks. This hardware-based approach provides a foundation for trusted computing in security-sensitive applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Mont-PrC is ideal for applications requiring hardware-level security assurance. Contact LiTong for security architecture consultation.

faqs.question: Is Jintide compatible with standard x86 software?
faqs.answer: Yes, Jintide CPUs are fully compatible with standard x86 software ecosystems. They support all standard x86 instructions and run operating systems including Windows Server, Linux, and VMware without modification. The security features operate transparently and do not require software changes for basic functionality. However, to fully leverage the security monitoring capabilities, Montage provides software tools and APIs for security management. Standard applications, databases, and virtualization platforms run without modification. Performance is comparable to mainstream x86 processors for most workloads.
faqs.decisionGuide: Jintide provides x86 compatibility with added security. Evaluate software compatibility for specialized applications.

faqs.question: How does Jintide enhance security compared to standard servers?
faqs.answer: Jintide enhances security through hardware-based monitoring that cannot be bypassed by software. Key advantages: Mont-PrC monitors CPU behavior at the hardware level; DSC provides real-time security monitoring without performance impact; Hardware encryption acceleration improves performance; Trusted execution environment support; TPM 2.0 integration for secure boot. Unlike software-based security solutions, Jintide's hardware monitoring operates independently of the operating system, making it immune to malware attacks that might disable software security tools.
faqs.decisionGuide: For applications requiring hardware-level security assurance, Jintide offers unique capabilities. Contact LiTong for security architecture consultation.

faqs.question: What is the performance impact of security monitoring?
faqs.answer: The Jintide platform's security monitoring has minimal performance impact on normal operations. Mont-PrC and DSC technologies are implemented in hardware and operate in parallel with CPU execution. The hardware encryption acceleration actually improves performance for encrypted workloads compared to software-only encryption. Real-world benchmarks show less than 2% performance overhead for security monitoring while providing significant acceleration for cryptographic operations. The platform delivers comparable performance to mainstream x86 processors for standard workloads.
faqs.decisionGuide: Jintide provides security without significant performance penalty. Evaluate for applications requiring both high performance and strong security.

faqs.question: What compliance standards does Jintide support?
faqs.answer: The Jintide platform supports various compliance standards including FIPS 140-2 for cryptographic modules, Common Criteria for security evaluation, and PCI DSS for payment card industry security. The hardware-based security monitoring helps meet requirements for intrusion detection and prevention. TPM 2.0 support enables trusted computing compliance. The platform can be configured to meet specific regulatory requirements for financial services, government, and healthcare applications. LiTong can provide compliance documentation and support certification efforts.
faqs.decisionGuide: Jintide supports major security compliance standards. Contact LiTong for specific compliance requirements and documentation.

products.name: Jintide C6P Secure Server CPU
products.shortDescription: High-performance secure server CPU with 64 cores and chip-level security monitoring
descriptionParagraphs: The Jintide C6P is a high-performance secure server CPU featuring 64 cores and integrated chip-level security monitoring.
Built on advanced process technology, it delivers exceptional compute performance while providing hardware-based security through Mont-PrC and DSC technologies.
The CPU is fully compatible with x86 software ecosystems while adding unique security capabilities for trusted computing applications.

faeReview.author: Dr. Sarah Liu
faeReview.title: Principal FAE - Secure Computing
faeReview.content: The Jintide C6P represents a unique approach to server security. Unlike software-based security solutions, the chip-level monitoring provided by Mont-PrC cannot be bypassed by malware or software attacks. I've worked with financial institutions and government agencies deploying these processors, and the security capabilities are impressive. Performance is comparable to mainstream x86 processors for most workloads. The hardware encryption acceleration provides significant performance benefits for encrypted workloads. One consideration: software optimized for specific x86 implementations may need validation, though standard applications run without modification. I highly recommend this platform for security-sensitive applications.
faeReview.highlight: Unique chip-level security monitoring that cannot be bypassed by software attacks

alternativeParts.reason: Intel Xeon offers mature ecosystem but lacks chip-level security monitoring
alternativeParts.useCase: Applications not requiring hardware-based security assurance

alternativeParts.reason: AMD offers more cores but lacks hardware security monitoring
alternativeParts.useCase: High-core-count applications not requiring hardware security

PCIe Gen5 retimer for I/O expansion
DDR5 DB for high-capacity memory

faqs.question: Is Jintide compatible with standard x86 software?
faqs.answer: Yes, Jintide CPUs are fully compatible with standard x86 software ecosystems. They support all standard x86 instructions and run operating systems including Windows Server, Linux, and VMware without modification. The security features operate transparently and do not require software changes for basic functionality. However, to fully leverage the security monitoring capabilities, Montage provides software tools and APIs for security management. Standard applications, databases, and virtualization platforms run without modification. Performance is comparable to mainstream x86 processors for most workloads.
faqs.decisionGuide: Jintide provides x86 compatibility with added security. Evaluate software compatibility for specialized applications.

faqs.question: What is Mont-PrC technology and how does it work?
faqs.answer: Mont-PrC (Montage Pre-Check) is a proprietary security technology that monitors CPU behavior at the hardware level. It tracks instruction execution patterns, memory access behaviors, and bus transactions in real-time. When anomalous patterns are detected - such as unexpected privilege escalations, unusual memory access patterns, or anomalous instruction sequences - the system can trigger alerts or protective actions. This hardware-based monitoring operates independently of the CPU's software state, making it impossible for malware to disable or bypass the monitoring. Mont-PrC provides a foundation for trusted computing that software solutions cannot match.
faqs.decisionGuide: Mont-PrC is ideal for applications requiring hardware-level threat detection. Contact LiTong for security architecture consultation.

faqs.question: How does Jintide performance compare to Intel Xeon and AMD EPYC?
faqs.answer: Jintide C6P performance is comparable to mainstream x86 processors for most workloads. The 64-core configuration with 2.5 GHz base frequency delivers strong compute performance. Benchmarks show competitive performance in integer, floating-point, and memory-intensive workloads. The hardware encryption acceleration provides significant advantages for encrypted workloads. Some specialized workloads optimized for specific Intel or AMD implementations may show differences, but standard applications run at comparable speeds. The key differentiator is the hardware security monitoring, which adds minimal performance overhead (< 2%).
faqs.decisionGuide: Jintide provides competitive performance with unique security features. Evaluate for your specific workload requirements.

faqs.question: What memory configurations are supported by Jintide C6P?
faqs.answer: The Jintide C6P supports DDR5-4800 memory with 12 channels. Maximum memory capacity depends on DIMM type: RDIMM configurations support up to 3TB per socket (12 x 256GB); LRDIMM configurations support up to 6TB per socket (12 x 512GB). The platform supports both registered and load-reduced DIMMs, allowing flexibility in capacity and cost. Memory operates at 1.1V for improved power efficiency. The 12-channel architecture provides exceptional memory bandwidth for data-intensive applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Plan memory configuration based on capacity and bandwidth requirements. Contact LiTong for memory compatibility verification.

faqs.question: How do I deploy Jintide in my data center?
faqs.answer: Deploying Jintide requires planning for both hardware integration and security configuration. Hardware integration follows standard x86 server design practices with attention to DDR5 memory routing and PCIe Gen5 signal integrity. Security configuration involves setting up Mont-PrC monitoring policies, configuring secure boot with TPM, and integrating with your security infrastructure. Montage provides reference designs, BIOS/firmware, and management tools. LiTong offers deployment support including design review, integration assistance, and security configuration guidance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong for deployment support and reference designs. Our FAE team can assist with integration and security configuration.

products.name: Jintide E24 Edge Server CPU
products.shortDescription: Edge-optimized secure server CPU with 24 cores for distributed computing applications
descriptionParagraphs: The Jintide E24 is an edge-optimized secure server CPU featuring 24 cores and integrated chip-level security monitoring.
Designed for edge computing and distributed applications, it delivers efficient performance with lower power consumption.
The CPU includes the same Mont-PrC and DSC security technologies as the C6P, providing hardware-based security for edge deployments.
features: Lower power consumption for edge deployments

faeReview.author: Dr. Sarah Liu
faeReview.title: Principal FAE - Secure Computing
faeReview.content: The Jintide E24 is an excellent choice for edge computing applications requiring security. The 24-core configuration provides sufficient performance for edge workloads while the 120W TDP enables efficient thermal design for edge environments. The same Mont-PrC security monitoring as the C6P ensures consistent security posture across core and edge deployments. I've worked with customers deploying these in smart city and industrial applications where security at the edge is critical. The lower power and PCIe Gen4 connectivity are well-suited for edge server designs. I recommend this for any edge application requiring hardware-level security.
faeReview.highlight: Edge-optimized design with hardware security for distributed deployments

alternativeParts.reason: Intel Xeon D offers edge-optimized design but lacks hardware security and DDR5 support
alternativeParts.useCase: Edge applications not requiring hardware security

alternativeParts.reason: AMD EPYC offers good performance but fewer cores and no hardware security

DDR5 RCD for server memory

faqs.question: When should I choose Jintide E24 over C6P?
faqs.answer: Choose Jintide E24 for: Edge computing deployments; Applications requiring lower power consumption (120W vs 250W); Workloads that fit within 24 cores; PCIe Gen4 is sufficient for I/O needs; Distributed or remote deployments. Choose Jintide C6P for: Data center deployments; Maximum compute performance required; Large memory capacity needed (12 channels vs 8); PCIe Gen5 required; High-density virtualization. The E24 is optimized for edge while C6P is optimized for data center performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select E24 for edge and distributed applications. Select C6P for data center performance requirements.

faqs.question: What is the maximum memory capacity for Jintide E24?
faqs.answer: The Jintide E24 supports DDR5-4400 memory with 8 channels. Maximum memory capacity: RDIMM configurations support up to 2TB (8 x 256GB); LRDIMM configurations support up to 4TB (8 x 512GB). The 8-channel architecture provides excellent memory bandwidth for edge workloads while maintaining lower power consumption than the 12-channel C6P. Memory operates at 1.1V for improved power efficiency.
faqs.decisionGuide: Plan memory configuration based on edge workload requirements. Contact LiTong for memory compatibility verification.

faqs.question: Does Jintide E24 have the same security features as C6P?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Jintide E24 includes the same core security features as the C6P: Mont-PrC chip-level security monitoring; DSC dynamic security monitoring; Hardware encryption acceleration; Trusted execution environment support. The security capabilities are identical - the E24 is optimized for power efficiency and edge deployment scenarios while maintaining full security functionality. Both processors provide hardware-based security monitoring that cannot be bypassed by software attacks.
faqs.decisionGuide: Both E24 and C6P provide identical security features. Choose based on performance and power requirements.

faqs.question: What thermal solutions work best for Jintide E24?
faqs.answer: The Jintide E24 with 120W TDP is well-suited for various thermal solutions: Standard air cooling with heatsink and fan is sufficient for most deployments; Passive heatsinks work in environments with adequate airflow; 1U server designs can accommodate the thermal requirements; Edge deployments can use compact cooling solutions. The lower TDP compared to C6P (250W) enables more flexible thermal designs. Thermal interface material and heatsink selection should follow standard practices for 120W processors.
faqs.decisionGuide: Standard air cooling is sufficient for most E24 deployments. Contact LiTong for thermal design recommendations.

faqs.question: What edge applications are best suited for Jintide E24?
faqs.answer: The Jintide E24 is ideal for edge applications requiring security: Smart city infrastructure with video analytics; Industrial IoT gateways with security monitoring; Remote office/branch office servers; Telecommunications edge computing; Autonomous vehicle infrastructure. The combination of x86 compatibility, hardware security, and optimized power makes it well-suited for distributed deployments where security cannot be compromised. The 24 cores provide sufficient performance for most edge workloads.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use E24 for edge applications requiring hardware security. Contact LiTong for application-specific recommendations.
